bottom_line: The workflow ran cleanly with zero thrash or failures across all 9 steps. The one actionable finding is that a code review step (S04) passed the implementation even though it was absent from the committed branch, suggesting the review examined the prompt output rather than the committed artifact.
coverage_notes: S01-S05 logs read in full (all < 50 lines). S06 (3662 lines) and S07 (3232 lines) sampled via grep + tail; no failures found. S08 and browser env logs read in full. DB telemetry confirmed UP throughout.

findings.title: Code review step issued PASS before code was committed to branch
findings.recommendation: Add a directive to the code-review-final prompt instructing the agent to run `git diff HEAD` to verify the committed artifact matches the expected implementation, before issuing a PASS verdict.
evidence: ai-dev/logs/CR-00071_S04_run1.log:1-41 — S04 code-review-final returned PASS with no findings
ai-dev/logs/CR-00071_S05_run1.log:1-12 — S05 code-review-fix-final discovered normalize_pi_messages was absent from the committed branch
orch/chat/context_usage.py:18 — normalize_pi_messages present in final worktree (added by S05)
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add git diff HEAD check to the code-review-final prompt template so reviewers verify the committed artifact, not just the prompt output; analyzed in CR-00071 (see ai-dev/work/CR-00071/reports/CR-00071_self_assess_report.md). Target file: ai-dev/templates/ChangeRequest_Design_Template.md. Effort: S.
